rationale: ARK Space Exploration & Innovation ETF launched March 30, 2021 (one day before as-of date) as a thematic vehicle for reusable rockets, satellites, drones, and orbital/sub-orbital aerospace exposure. With SpaceX still private and pure-play space names scarce, ARKX is the cleanest liquid public entry point for the rockets-and-spacecraft next wave the profile calls out. Debut day saw ~$294M traded (8th-best ETF debut on record), so liquidity is adequate.

Taiwan Semiconductor announced a record 2021 capex plan of $25-28B (up 45-62% YoY) on January 14, 2021, explicitly to expand leading-edge 5nm/3nm capacity for AI and HPC customers. TSMC is the sole-source foundry for Nvidia, AMD, and Apple silicon — every AI training and inference workload routes through its fabs. Q1 2021 reporting also showed industry-wide capacity exceeding demand, which is bullish for the foundry monopolist. Adds an AI-wave exposure that is upstream of NVDA (already in watchlist) and harder to disrupt.

Intuitive Surgical is the dominant surgical-robotics franchise (da Vinci installed base ~5,989 systems at end-2020, recurring instruments/services revenue). Profile calls out robotics as a next wave and aging-population demographics as a non-tech wave — ISRG sits at the intersection. Q4 2020 earnings (released Jan 2021) showed procedure volumes recovering from COVID disruption. Provides liquid US-listed robotics exposure without taking on the operating risk of speculative humanoid or industrial-automation names that mostly aren't public yet.

Broad-market index largely duplicates exposure already held via AAPL/MSFT/GOOGL/NVDA (top SPY weights). Profile prioritizes early-wave concentration over benchmark hugging; freeing the slot creates room for nuclear-wave entry.

Heavy AI-semi overlap with NVDA on the same chip-shortage / TSMC-capex tailwind, plus Taiwan single-country concentration risk that is not the geopolitical exposure the profile is trying to express. Trimming TSM keeps the AI wave covered via NVDA and frees a slot for a defense/geopolitical add.

Diluted space-thesis vehicle: top holdings are split across defense primes, aerospace incumbents, and tangential infrastructure rather than pure-play launch/spacecraft names. Replacing with RKLB gives a cleaner read on the rockets next-wave bet without changing the slot's wave bucket.

Pure-play trapped-ion quantum computing operator; only US-listed quantum pure-play available at this date. IPO'd via SPAC on NYSE October 2021 (listing date 2021-10-01 — pre-as-of, eligible), raised $636M. Fits the profile's named 'quantum' next-wave bucket where the universe of investable pure-plays is otherwise empty. Profile guidance is to invest early in next waves; quantum is pre-commercial today, so this is a small-position high-asymmetry slot, not a core allocation. Replaces AAPL to stay at cap.

Trim to make room for a quantum pure-play (IONQ). Among the four AI-megacap names in the watchlist (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA), AAPL has the least direct exposure to the generative-AI / accelerated-compute build-out: MSFT carries Azure and enterprise AI distribution, GOOGL carries TPU/search/cloud, NVDA is the accelerator monopoly. AAPL is a mature consumer-hardware franchise — profile guidance 'ride AI wave, trim before crest' applies most cleanly to the megacap with the least incremental AI upside per dollar. Note: not a thesis-negative call on AAPL, just the lowest-marginal-AI name in a capped basket.

Eli Lilly fills the profile's named non-tech next wave (demographics / chronic-disease of aging populations) which the cap-8 basket currently does not cover. Mounjaro (tirzepatide) received FDA approval on 2022-05-13 for type-2 diabetes with a strong weight-loss readout already in the SURPASS trial program, opening a multi-decade obesity/metabolic franchise tied to aging demographics in the US, EU, and Japan. LLY is a large-cap US-listed liquid name with multi-decade trading history. Not engineered biology — tirzepatide is a peptide GLP-1/GIP dual agonist, classical pharma chemistry, not cell or genome engineering.

Fills the geopolitical-energy gap the profile names explicitly (Russia-Ukraine driving oil/gas/LNG); Saudi+Russia extending voluntary production cuts pushed WTI to ~$90 and XLE to a Sept-2023 peak of ~$92.95, a multi-quarter structural setup the current watchlist has zero exposure to.

Re-fills the rockets/spacecraft gap that opened in Q3 when RKLB was removed after the Sept 19 Electron failure; SpaceX's two 2023 Starship integrated test flights (April 20 IFT-1, Nov 18 IFT-2) plus a growing commercial-launch backlog mark a clean buildup phase for the wave the profile explicitly names, and an ETF wrapper diversifies single-vehicle/launch-failure risk that single-stock RKLB carried.

Pure-play quantum-computing equities remain pre-revenue speculation at year-end 2023 with fault-tolerant quantum advantage still many years out; IONQ specifically saw execution wobble in 2023 (the company replaced its CEO in May 2023 and the stock has been volatile on a still-untested commercial roadmap), so the quantum slot in a tight 8-cap watchlist is better redeployed into a wave with concrete 2023 milestones (rockets/spacecraft). Quantum exposure can be revisited when fault-tolerance milestones or a broader quantum-themed ETF make the wave less single-name speculative.

Adds dedicated robotics exposure to a watchlist whose only robotics name is medical-only (ISRG); NVIDIA's GR00T humanoid-foundation-model launch at GTC (Mar 18, 2024) is a credible buildup-phase catalyst for the broader humanoid/industrial robotics wave the profile names explicitly.

Pairs the BOTZ add to keep |watchlist|=8; thesis breach signal — Gemini image-generation rollout was paused mid-quarter after a public backlash, ~$70-96B of market cap was shed in days, and Alphabet now visibly trails MSFT/OpenAI on consumer-AI execution while MSFT and NVDA remain in the watchlist to carry the AI buildup wave.

Fills the watchlist's most glaring thesis gap — quantum computing — via a diversified ETF basket (IBM, GOOGL, HON, IONQ, RGTI) as quantum hardware milestones (IBM Heron 133-qubit processor, Google's below-threshold error-correction result) start to look like a real buildup phase.

Integrated-oil-heavy energy ETF (XOM/CVX ~40%) is a noisy expression of the user's geopolitical-energy thesis, which is really about LNG and Russia-Ukraine flows; crude has rolled over from April highs and the diversified-majors basket is not where the thesis edge lives.

Hyperscaler-nuclear PPAs are the clearest near-term commercial signal for the user's nuclear-SMR thesis this quarter; NLR's mix of uranium miners plus nuclear utilities (Constellation, Vistra, PG&E, Public Service) captures the utility leg that URA (pure miners) misses.

Pure-play US LNG exporter Cheniere brought its Corpus Christi Stage 3 expansion online in December 2024, adding capacity right as Europe's reliance on US LNG deepened with the Ukraine-Russia gas transit agreement set to expire Jan 1, 2025 - filling the geopolitical-energy bucket the profile names but the watchlist currently does not cover.

ARKX is a diluted rockets/spacecraft vehicle (top holdings include Trimble, Iridium, Kratos - more aero/comms than pure launch); we trim it now to make cap room for a higher-conviction geopolitical-energy add and can re-express rockets exposure via a pure-play (e.g., RKLB) in a later quarter when warranted.

Rocket Lab signed five Neutron contracts plus a $190M / 20-launch HASTE order in Q1 2025 and is tracking a Neutron debut in 2H 2025 - the pure-play rockets/spacecraft exposure the profile names as a 'next wave' and which the watchlist has had zero coverage of since ARKX was removed last quarter.

BOTZ is a diluted robotics ETF (top holdings span industrial automation conglomerates and Japanese factory-automation names) whose constituents do not really map to the rapid-iteration robotics wave the profile is targeting; ISRG remains as the pure-play robotics exposure, and dropping BOTZ frees a cap slot for a higher-conviction rockets pure-play (RKLB) - same logic as the ARKX-for-LNG swap last quarter.
